messages.content: Ein Gebäude wird so geplant, dass sich gleichzeitig 120 Personen darin aufhalten können. Es ist davon auszugehen, dass sich gleich viele Männer wie Frauen im Gebäude befinden und die Toiletten kontinuierlich benutzt werden. Wie viele weibliche Sitztoiletten, männliche Sitztoiletten und Urinalstände?

Building on your previous question regarding the required number of sanitary facilities for a building designed for 120 people (with equal numbers of men and women and continuous use), the relevant Austrian regulations are primarily found in the OIB Guidelines (OIB-Richtlinie 4: Nutzungssicherheit und Barrierefreiheit) and the Austrian Standard ÖNORM B 1600 (Barrierefreies Bauen). According to these standards:

- For women: 1 Sitztoilette (WC) per 15 women → 4 Sitztoiletten
- For men: 1 Sitztoilette per 50 men → 2 Sitztoiletten
- For men: 1 Urinal per 25 men → 3 Urinale

Therefore, for 120 persons (60 women, 60 men), the minimum requirements are:
- 4 female Sitztoiletten
- 2 male Sitztoiletten
- 3 Urinalstände

These figures reflect the minimum legal requirements for public buildings in Austria and are based on the most recent versions of OIB-Richtlinie 4 and ÖNORM B 1600. Additionally, ÖNORM B 1600 specifies technical requirements for barrier-free WC rooms (e.g., minimum room size of 185 cm x 215 cm for accessible toilets). No images or diagrams specifically illustrating toilet counts were found in the retrieved documents, but technical diagrams regarding barrier-free dimensions are present in ÖNORM B 1600. If you need further details on special cases (e.g., schools, gastronomy) or want the exact legal text, please specify.

References:
- OIB-Richtlinie 4 (2023 edition)
- ÖNORM B 1600:2017-04-01 (Barrier-Free Construction)
- Document: 01_Data base documents/04_ÖNORM/4_AT_0_0_OEN_B 1600 Barrierefreies Bauen.pdf (p. 43)
